title: Intensely Roasted German Leek Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful German stew featuring roasted leeks, perfect for a weeknight dinner, cooked in a donabe vessel and gluten-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: leeks
ingredients.notes: white and light green parts only

instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the leeks in half lengthwise and rinse them under cold water to remove any dirt.
Dry the leeks with paper towels and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the leeks with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Roast the leeks in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and caramelized.
Remove the leeks from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Slice the roasted leeks into 1-inch pieces and set them aside.
In the donabe vessel, cook the sliced pork sausage over medium heat until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the donabe and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the donabe, cooking for 5 minutes, or until they start to soften.
Add the roasted leeks, chicken broth, and browned sausage to the donabe, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a simmer and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
